title: pseudopodospermum idaeum
intro: Pseudopodospermum idaeum, a plant species in the family Anacardiaceae, is also known as Idaea or Indian walnut, and is native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant's bark and leaves are used in traditional medicine, and it is believed to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Pseudopodospermum idaeum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and anti-dysenteric agent. The plant's bark and leaves can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
